title: UCP Profile Document
description: Variant-neutral wrapper schema for UCP profile documents. Use the business_schema definition to validate business profiles and the platform_schema definition to validate platform profiles.

jwk_public_key.description: Public JSON Web Key used for HTTP Message Signatures and signed webhook verification. UCP profiles publish public keys only; private key material MUST NOT appear in a profile. UCP supports two key types: EC (ECDSA P-256, P-384) and OKP (Ed25519); OKP keys are RECOMMENDED for HTTP transport identity when Web Bot Auth (WBA) interop is desired. A single profile MAY publish keys of either or both types; consumers select keys by kid. Additional public JWK members are permitted; consumers ignore unknown members.

oneOf.title: EC Public Key (ECDSA)
oneOf.description: Elliptic curve public key per RFC 7518 §6.2.

crv.description: Elliptic curve name. P-256 pairs with ES256; P-384 pairs with ES384.

alg.description: JWA algorithm associated with this public key. Optional; verifiers derive the algorithm from crv when alg is omitted. When present, MUST match crv: ES256 with P-256, ES384 with P-384.

oneOf.title: OKP Public Key (EdDSA)
oneOf.description: Octet key pair public key per RFC 8037 (CFRG curves).

crv.description: CFRG curve name. UCP supports Ed25519; the algorithm is EdDSA per RFC 8037.

alg.description: JWA algorithm associated with this public key. Optional; verifiers derive the algorithm from crv when alg is omitted.

kid.description: Key identifier referenced by Signature-Input keyid. Profiles MAY use any string. For Web Bot Auth-compatible signatures, the JWK SHA-256 Thumbprint (RFC 7638) is RECOMMENDED.

kty.description: JWK key type. EC for ECDSA (P-256, P-384); OKP for EdDSA (Ed25519).

use.description: JWK public key use. UCP examples use sig for signatures.

base.description: Common wrapper for UCP profile documents.

ucp.description: Protocol metadata, capabilities, services, and payment handlers advertised by this party.

signing_keys.description: Canonical UCP profile field for publishing signing keys. When a profile publishes signing keys, they MUST appear here; this is where every UCP verifier reads them. A future UCP version may promote keys[] to canonical; profiles wanting WBA interop today MAY publish both arrays as mirrors.

keys.description: Optional top-level JWK Set per RFC 7517, mirroring signing_keys[]. When present, makes the UCP profile a valid JWK Set, enabling the same document to also serve as a Web Bot Auth-compatible key directory. WBA-shape verifiers reading the profile URL via Signature-Agent locate keys here; UCP-only verifiers continue to read from signing_keys[]. When both arrays are published, they MUST list the same set of kid values, and entries sharing a kid MUST be semantically equivalent JWKs (same members and values; serialization differences not significant). See the Deployment Patterns for WBA Interop section in the overview for hosting patterns.

business_schema.title: UCP Business Profile Document
business_schema.description: Profile document hosted by a business at /.well-known/ucp.

platform_schema.title: UCP Platform Profile Document
platform_schema.description: Profile document hosted by a platform and advertised to businesses via UCP-Agent.
